name: Apricot and Cherry Country Tart
recipeIngredient: 1 cup pitted cherries or frozen, thawed
2 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted and cooled
Vanilla ice cream or frozen yogurt
recipeInstructions:
FOR CRUST: Combine flour, 1/8 teaspoon sugar, and salt in a bowl. Add chilled butter; rub into the mixture with fingertips until it forms a coarse meal. Mix in enough water, tablespoon by tablespoon, until the dough comes together in clumps. Form into a ball, place on parchment paper, wrap in plastic, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour up to 2 days.
Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Roll out the dough on a floured surface until it forms an 11-inch round. Transfer the rolled-out dough onto the prepared baking sheet.
FOR FILLING: In a bowl, combine 2 tablespoons flour and sugar. Sprinkle over the crust, leaving a 1 1/2-inch border. Arrange apricots cut-side down on the crust, spacing them closely and leaving a 1-1/2 inch border at the outer edge. Scatter cherries over the apricots.
Sprinkle 4 tablespoons sugar over the fruit. Fold the pastry edges up around the apricots, pressing against them to form a scalloped border. Brush the crust with melted butter and sprinkle an additional tablespoon of sugar on top.
Bake until the crust is golden brown and the fruit is tender (some juices will run onto the parchment), about 1 hour. Remove from oven using a pastry brush to wipe away excess juice on the parchment. Gently slide the tart onto a rack, being careful not to disturb the crust as it is fragile. Once cool, carefully transfer the tart to a serving platter and serve with ice cream or yogurt.
